channel.title: Open Free Books
channel.description: Recent updates to Open Free Books pages and live chapters.

item.title: Integer foundations
item.description: Model directed magnitude on number lines; use the zero principle and tiles to add and subtract integers, and see subtraction as adding an opposite.

item.title: Integer arithmetic
item.description: Multiply and divide integers from models to symbolic rules; apply order of operations with signed numbers in multi-step expressions.

item.title: Percentage applications
item.description: Relate fractions and decimals to base-100 ratios; calculate percent increase and decrease in financial and real-world contexts.

item.title: Fractions and decimals
item.description: Convert between fractions, terminating decimals, and repeating decimals; add, subtract, multiply, and divide rational numbers and mixed numbers.

item.title: Ratios & rates foundations
item.description: Formulate part-to-part and part-to-whole ratios, find equivalent ratios, compute unit rates, and graph proportional relationships.

item.title: Advanced trigonometry
item.description: Use sine and cosine rules, radians, and trigonometric graphs in 2D and 3D applications.

item.title: Basic probability
item.description: Calculate theoretical and experimental probabilities for simple events and complements.

item.title: Properties of circles
item.description: Apply chord, tangent, and angle theorems in circles to prove results and solve geometry problems.

item.title: Congruence & similarity
item.description: Apply congruence and similarity criteria to justify properties of triangles and scale figures.

item.title: Coordinate geometry of circles
item.description: Find equations of circles, tangents, and intersections using coordinate methods and distance.

item.title: Coordinate geometry foundations
item.description: Plot points, find distances and midpoints, and work with slopes of lines in the Cartesian plane.

item.title: Deductive geometry
item.description: Prove angle and side relationships for intersecting lines, triangles, and polygons.

item.title: Introduction to trigonometry
item.description: Define sine, cosine, and tangent ratios and solve right-angled triangle problems.

item.title: Loci
item.description: Describe and construct loci in the plane, linking geometric constraints to algebraic equations.

item.title: Measures of dispersion
item.description: Interpret range, interquartile range, variance, and standard deviation for comparing data sets.

item.title: Mensuration in 3D
item.description: Find volumes and surface areas of prisms, cylinders, pyramids, cones, and spheres.

item.title: Normal distribution & z-scores
item.description: Model continuous data with the normal curve, standardise with z-scores, and read probabilities from tables.

item.title: Permutations, combinations & probability
item.description: Count outcomes with permutations and combinations, then compute probabilities in finite sample spaces.

item.title: Pythagoras' theorem
item.description: Use Pythagoras' theorem and its converse in 2D problems and coordinate settings.

item.title: Charts & averages
item.description: Organise data with tables and charts and calculate mean, median, and mode.

item.title: Statistical literacy
item.description: Evaluate claims in media and research: sampling bias, correlation versus causation, and misleading charts.

item.title: Complex numbers
item.description: Represent complex numbers in Cartesian form and perform operations including conjugates and division.

item.title: Exponential & logarithmic functions
item.description: Use exponential and logarithmic models, apply log laws, and solve growth and decay equations.

item.title: Factor & remainder theorems
item.description: Use the factor and remainder theorems to factorise higher-degree polynomials and solve related equations.

item.title: Functions & graphs
item.description: Model input-output rules, work with domain and range, and sketch key function graphs and transformations.

item.title: Linear equations in two unknowns
item.description: Solve pairs of linear equations by substitution, elimination, and graphs; classify unique, parallel, and coincident cases.

item.title: Linear programming
item.description: Graph linear inequalities, identify feasible regions, and optimise objective functions in two variables.

item.title: Sequences & series
item.description: Work with arithmetic and geometric sequences, sigma notation, and finite summation formulas.

item.title: Variations
item.description: Model direct, inverse, joint, and partial variation and solve combined variation problems.

item.title: Laws of indices
item.description: Apply index laws to simplify expressions with integer and rational exponents.

item.title: Linear inequalities in one unknown
item.description: Solve, represent, and apply linear inequalities in one unknown using number lines and interval notation.

item.title: Counting and numbers
item.description: Count objects, spot patterns, add, multiply, and divide; then meet place value and big numbers from hundreds to billions.

item.title: Written calculation
item.description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide whole numbers using vertical written form: align columns, regroup, and read the result.

item.title: FAQ
item.description: FAQ

item.title: Physics
item.description: Browse physics chapters in the curriculum catalog.

item.title: Force and Newton's laws
item.description: Use free-body diagrams, F = ma, and motion graphs to predict how objects move.

item.title: Basic math notation
item.description: Learn the names and meanings of plus, minus, times, divide, equals, brackets, and other signs used in number sentences.

item.title: Zero and one
item.description: 0 is nothing; 1 is one. Learn to tell them apart — the same skill as telling false from true — before counting larger amounts.

item.title: Algebraic fractions & formulas
item.description: Simplify and combine algebraic fractions; rearrange formulas in science contexts.

item.title: Polynomials: expansion & factorisation
item.description: Expand products, collect like terms, and factorise expressions using common factors and identities.

item.title: Properties of operations
item.description: See why order, grouping, and distribution can change how you write a sum or product without changing the result — with interactive area and dot models.

item.title: Quadratic equations
item.description: The discriminant, nature of roots, and the graph of y = ax² + bx + c.

item.title: Search
item.description: Search

item.title: Contribute
item.description: Contribute

item.title: Mathematics
item.description: Browse mathematics chapters in the curriculum catalog.

item.title: Catalog
item.description: Catalog

item.title: Manifesto
item.description: Manifesto

item.title: Credits
item.description: Credits

item.title: Open Free Books
item.description: Free, open-source textbooks for every learner — elementary through university. Read the full book online at no cost, or contribute on GitHub.
